title: FX Brief — 02 Jul 2026
intro:
Sterling surges on broad-based strength while yen weakness persists despite minor USD softness in thin Asian trading.
💪 CURRENCY RANKING
GBP +0.82% — Momentum from late June rally continues, outperforming across all pairs with strong technical breakouts
NZD +0.58% — Risk-on sentiment and carry appeal support kiwi despite muted Asian data flow
EUR +0.26% — Modest gains vs USD but significant underperformance against GBP weighing on cross
JPY -0.27% — Persistent weakness continues near multi-decade lows at 162.24, intervention risks mounting
CHF +0.26% — Safe haven bid provides support as European uncertainty lingers
AUD -0.05% — Commodity currency lags despite stable risk sentiment, China concerns capping upside
CAD -0.07% — Energy prices fail to lift loonie, underperforms vs major peers
USD -0.024% — Neutral tone in holiday-thinned Asian session, minor weakness across majors

sections.heading: 📊 FX THEME
sections.content:
Sterling surges on broad-based strength while yen weakness persists despite minor USD softness in thin Asian trading.
💪 CURRENCY RANKING
GBP +0.82% — Momentum from late June rally continues, outperforming across all pairs with strong technical breakouts
NZD +0.58% — Risk-on sentiment and carry appeal support kiwi despite muted Asian data flow
EUR +0.26% — Modest gains vs USD but significant underperformance against GBP weighing on cross
JPY -0.27% — Persistent weakness continues near multi-decade lows at 162.24, intervention risks mounting
CHF +0.26% — Safe haven bid provides support as European uncertainty lingers
AUD -0.05% — Commodity currency lags despite stable risk sentiment, China concerns capping upside
CAD -0.07% — Energy prices fail to lift loonie, underperforms vs major peers
USD -0.024% — Neutral tone in holiday-thinned Asian session, minor weakness across majors

sections.heading: 🎯 TRADE IDEAS
sections.content:
LONG: GBPUSD | 1.3280 | 1.3240 | 1.3380 | Momentum continuation, technical breakout above 1.3250 resistance
LONG: GBPJPY | 215.50 | 214.20 | 218.50 | Dual tailwinds from sterling strength and yen weakness, carry advantage
SHORT: EURGBP | 0.8575 | 0.8620 | 0.8480 | Sterling outperformance intact, eurozone growth concerns vs UK resilience
SHORT: AUDUSD | 0.6905 | 0.6950 | 0.6820 | China slowdown fears, technical resistance holding, USD stabilization potential

sections.heading: ⚠️ RISKS
sections.content: BOJ intervention threat acute above 162.50 USDJPY could trigger sharp yen snapback. Thin holiday liquidity amplifies volatility risk into US Independence Day tomorrow.
